author: $UPER-NODES.io
msg: What do you mean exactly? Hopefully my questions will be answered during next weeks UNO-AMA.

author: $UPER-NODES.io
msg:
#amaquestions
1) Are the rewards that are currently being earned per ULO mainly rewards for being online and for a certain uptime or is the Unity App performing real customer assignments? If assignments what assignments are being done? 2) What are the tiers (#) of ULO’s perIP-address (or SIM) before the rewards are reduced? 3) I would like to propose the possibility to run more ULO’s (say max 120 ULO’s) per IP-address before the rewards per ULO will be punished. 120 ULO < 0,01% of the available ULO. This to keep word with the earlier stated FAQ-question (see below) and to make it possible to run a small company. For me max 0,01% per IP is still superdecentralized and distributed! What is your idea about this proposal? Q: Are there any limits on how many Unity Operator Software licenses canbe operated from a single location? A: There is no limit to the number of Unity License Operators that canrun from a single location. However, we do not encourage this. The networkfunctions most efficiently and effectively when it is as decentralized andgeographically distributed as possible. 4) What happened with IOS PoW? 5) When is fiat currency cash out possible? 6) Where is the Potential Monthly Rewards Calculator (Up to $19,200 per Node) based upon? I can’t imagine it is pure fantasy or pure guessing… 7) What has happened and will happen to Zebec Debit Card– both for Unity and WorldMobile?
